Djamé Seddah

Research in NLP, Robust Language Modeling, LLM and Model Safety, Low Ressource Languages and Specialized domains

Home Research People Publications Models and Datasets Teaching Contact

Teaching

I was a tenured associate professor in CS at la Sorbonne Universisty (ex Paris IV) until Feb. 2018.

I don’t teach much anymore except for two classes at Univ. Paris Cité where I teach 6 lectures (half a semester) to the M2 and talk about LLMs and How to prevent their weaponisation (20HETD).

I also teach an advanced NLP class targeted to profesional project managers in the automotive domain at Paris Dauphine (21HETD) as part of my PRAIRIE-PSAI engagment.

Typical teaching topics at la Sorbonne

  • Models for computational linguistics (M1)
  • Generic and object-oriented programming for NLP (M1)
  • Introduction to syntax-based machine translation (M2)
  • Java programming (L2)

Pedagogical responsibilities

  • Director of studies for the C2I program at Université Paris-Sorbonne

Earlier course portfolio (during my PhD tiùe)

Computer science

  • C programming
  • Algorithms
  • Operating systems
  • Mathematical algorithms
  • CAML programming

NLP and linguistics

  • Linguistic tools
  • Distributed applications
  • Syntax
  • Morphology
  • Introduction to linguistics
  • Linguistics for communication
